Title: Manipulation of ZDS in tomato exposes carotenoid- and ABA-specific effects on fruit development and ripening

Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Early fruit development and ripening stages
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates fruit development, plastid division, fruit size and ripening timing
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Defective SlNCED1 in notabilis lowers ABA and serves as an ABA-deficient control with delayed ripening, altered chloroplast division and reduced fruit size
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Use of notabilis mutant control; ABA quantification; fruit development phenotyping; qRT-PCR of downstream marker genes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. notabilis/SlNCED1 deficiency causes reduced ABA during fruit development. 2. The mutant phenocopies several developmental effects seen in ZDS-repressed fruit, helping separate ABA-specific from carotenoid-specific effects. 3. ABA deficiency delays ripening and reduces fruit size.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reference gene for modulating ABA-mediated fruit development and ripening, but reduced ABA has clear negative fruit-size and ripening tradeoffs
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced fruit size, delayed ripening and other ABA-deficiency traits
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used primarily as a comparator control rather than the main engineered target, but experimentally central to interpreting ZDS-to-ABA phenotypes.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plastid division protein; tubulin-like GTPase family
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in early developing fruit of ZDS-RNAi and not lines
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Early fruit developmental stages under ABA deficiency
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Affects chloroplast/plastid number, influencing pigment accumulation capacity
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated under ZDS repression and ABA deficiency, consistent with increased chloroplast division in developing fruit
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in developing fruit across genotypes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FtsZ transcript abundance rises in ZDS-repressed and not fruit. 2. This supports increased plastid division as an ABA-deficiency response. 3. FtsZ helps explain developmental pigment phenotypes downstream of ZDS manipulation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential plastid-number marker when engineering carotenoid/ABA-related fruit traits
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changing plastid division may have broad consequences for fruit development and metabolism
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was experimentally measured as a mechanistic mediator of ABA-related developmental effects downstream of ZDS.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Carotenoid biosynthesis; ABA biosynthesis via carotenoid precursors
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core carotenoid desaturation module upstream of lycopene formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: neurosporene intermediates en route to all-trans-lycopene; all-trans-ζ-carotene isomer pool changes reported
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces additional desaturation into ζ-carotene isomers during conversion toward all-trans-lycopene, acting with CRTISO in the linear carotenoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms precursor to target product and is required for downstream lycopene, β-carotene, xanthophyll and ABA accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in all aerial tissues; highest in leaves, ripening fruit and flowers; induced at ripening initiation and through early ripening
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in leaves, flowers and ripening fruit; dynamic during fruit development with strong induction at breaker/early ripening
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for RNAi repression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low-light growth followed by transfer to natural/normal light revealed transient photobleaching in leaves; fruit development and ripening stages were critical contexts
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Lycopene determines ripe fruit red color; β-carotene/xanthophylls provide photoprotection; ABA regulates seed germination, fruit development, fruit size and ripening timing
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Ripe fruit chromoplasts; also flowers and green aerial tissues for photoprotective carotenoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Repression blocks carotenoid biosynthesis downstream of ζ-carotene, causing accumulation of upstream carotenoids, loss of lycopene, reduced β-carotene/xanthophylls, lowered ABA, photobleached immature fruit, delayed ripening and smaller fruit; overexpression modestly increases ripe-fruit lycopene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Constitutive RNAi repression in tomato; heterologous overexpression of Arabidopsis ZDS in tomato; qRT-PCR; HPLC carotenoid profiling; LC-MS ABA quantification; ripening/fruit-size phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as an additional bottleneck/minor limitation in ripening-associated carotenogenesis, though PSY1 remains the major rate-limiting step
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq and microarray data were referenced from public tomato resources, but no accession number reported; metabolite profiling by HPLC, LC-MS, and GC-MS in this study
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlZDS is a single-copy tomato carotenoid biosynthetic gene strongly induced during ripening. 2. SlZDS repression causes ζ-carotene and upstream carotenoid accumulation, severe depletion of lycopene and reduced downstream photoprotective carotenoids and ABA. 3. Reduced SlZDS delays ripening, lowers fruit mass and alters fruit quality traits, while heterologous ZDS overexpression modestly elevates lycopene, indicating SlZDS is an additional bottleneck in ripening carotenogenesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A useful engineering target to fine-tune tomato carotenoid flux and ripening timing; especially promising for stacking with upstream genes such as PDS/PSY1 when substrate supply is elevated. Strong repression causes major developmental penalties, so partial upregulation rather than knockout would be preferable.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong repression causes severe dwarfism/homozygous lethality-like propagation failure, fruit photobleaching, lower ABA, smaller fruit, delayed ripening, reduced soluble solids and altered ethylene dynamics
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tomato appears to carry a single ZDS copy, explaining pleiotropic effects. Endogenous tomato ZDS overexpression was avoided because of co-suppression risk; AtZDS bypassed this issue.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core carotenoid desaturation module upstream of lycopene formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes desaturation of ζ-carotene intermediates toward lycopene in the linear carotenoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms precursor to lycopene and relieves substrate bottleneck
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene highly expressed in young leaves, anthesis flowers and especially chromoplast-rich ripe fruits
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening stage (7 DPB) was key to observing metabolic effects
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to ripe fruit pigmentation and nutritional carotenoid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Heterologous overexpression in tomato modestly increases ripe-fruit lycopene and shifts ζ-carotene isomer composition, depleting 9,9′-di-cis-ζ-carotene and 9-cis-ζ-carotene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic overexpression; qRT-PCR; HPLC carotenoid profiling; fruit phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supports ZDS as an additional bottleneck/minor limitation in ripening-associated tomato carotenogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis ZDS can function in tomato to bypass endogenous co-suppression issues. 2. AtZDS overexpression modestly elevates ripe-fruit lycopene without major effects in leaves or flowers. 3. The transgene alters ζ-carotene isomer balance, revealing ZDS as a modest bottleneck during tomato ripening.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential transgenic/metabolic-engineering tool to increase tomato lycopene, particularly when combined with enhanced upstream precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Generally limited phenotypic effects; one line showed co-suppression-like phenotype during T0, highlighting transgene design caution
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used heterologously to avoid tomato endogenous ZDS co-suppression.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes ZDS expression during ripening; ZDS transcripts are reduced in rin mutant fruit
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important ripening regulator contributing to transcriptional induction of ZDS and thereby carotenoid accumulation in ripening fruit
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ZDS promoter binding supported by cited ChIP-seq database mining
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ripening-associated fruit regulator context at 7 DPB
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Fruit ripening developmental program; ethylene-related ripening network
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening stage comparison in rin mutant versus wild type
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Ripe fruit pigmentation and nutritional quality
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced RIN activity lowers ZDS transcripts, consistent with reduced ripening-associated carotenoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in rin mutant fruit; promoter binding inference from public ChIP-seq resources
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RIN positively contributes to ZDS transcription during ripening. 2. ZDS expression is reduced in rin mutant fruit. 3. RIN is part of the regulatory network driving carotenoid accumulation in ripening tomato fruit.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Indirect target for modifying ripening-associated carotenoid accumulation, though broad pleiotropic ripening effects require caution
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad ripening regulator with likely extensive pleiotropic effects beyond carotenoids
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core regulator because the paper experimentally tested its effect on ZDS transcript accumulation in ripening mutants.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Strongly promotes ZDS expression during ripening; nor showed the greatest reduction in ZDS transcripts
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Highlighted as especially influential for ZDS regulation and ripening-associated carotenoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ZDS promoter binding supported by cited ChIP-seq database mining
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ripening-associated fruit regulator context at 7 DPB
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Fruit ripening developmental program; ethylene-related ripening network
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening stage comparison in nor mutant versus wild type
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Ripe fruit pigmentation and nutritional quality
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of NOR function causes the strongest reduction in ZDS expression among tested ripening mutants, indicating strong positive control of carotenoid biosynthesis via ZDS
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in nor mutant fruit; promoter binding inference from public ChIP-seq resources
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NOR is a major positive regulator of ZDS expression during ripening. 2. nor fruit show the greatest ZDS transcript reduction among tested ripening mutants. 3. NOR is likely a direct regulator of ZDS based on promoter-binding data mining.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential lever for tuning ripening carotenoids, but manipulation would strongly affect whole-ripening programs
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong pleiotropy on ripening and development expected
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper specifically highlights NOR as influential in ZDS regulation.
